sections.paragraph: σϰληροϰαρδία, ας, ἡ [σκληρός, καρδία] ‘refusal to budge from a point of view’, stubbornness, perversity, wrongheadedness Mt 19:8; Mk 10:5; 16:14. (In Engl. a transliteration such as ‘hardness of heart’ ordinarily denotes insensitivity, but this is not the central idea in the NT Greek texts nor in the LXX.)
